source_idea.paper_title: CODA: Rewriting Transformer Blocks as GEMM-Epilogue Programs
source_idea.read: The paper's useful abstraction is GEMM mainloop plus programmable epilogue: keep tile results on chip, apply local transforms/reductions while the output tile is still resident, and avoid writing intermediate probability or gradient slabs to global memory.

gemm_mainloops: dP^T @ Q + P^T @ dO -> compressed dKV contributions
epilogue_work: Use scores, lse, and delta to produce P and scaled dP without materializing HSK probs or grad_logits.
Route selected slots to compressed group ids.
Reduce contributions for repeated compressed groups inside the tile before global writes.
Emit reduced atomics or compact partial rows instead of one atomic/store per selected row and D element.
mapping_to_sparse_mla_backward.target_contract: q, dO, shared compressed KV, source indices, lse, delta -> dq, dKV, dsink with no selected_kv, probs, or grad_logits HBM materialization.

implemented_probe.name: dblocked_grouped_source
implemented_probe.description: A TileLang direct-source grouped-D reducer that recomputes P/dP once per token and selected tile, then reuses it for multiple D blocks inside the same CTA before reduced dKV atomic writes.

artifacts.read: Functional smoke passed closely enough for BF16 bridge bring-up, but tiny-shape timing is already negative.
This is too slow before attempting S4096 or e2e TFLOPs promotion.

compile_or_shape_notes: H=16 smoke failed earlier in the existing TileLang no-dKV helper with ROCm layout inference divide-by-zero.
block_d=32 failed ROCm layout inference for the new grouped-D reducer; block_d=64 compiled.
TileLang emitted a data-race warning on group_acc for the grouped-D reducer, so the current lowering is not production-safe yet.

comparison_to_existing_gates.read: The grouped-D direct-source probe is conceptually closer to GEMM+epilogue because it avoids HSK materialization, but it is far slower than both the old Triton sparse-atomic wall and the source-fed HSK TileLang dKV body.

decision.summary: Use GEMM+epilogue as the design target, but do not promote this current TileLang grouped-D implementation. It proves wiring and the right no-HSK contract direction, yet still pays too much recompute/schedule overhead and has a TileLang race warning.
next_branch: If staying in TileLang, rewrite the kernel so P/dP is produced once per query-major MFMA tile and consumed by an epilogue-local segmented/group reduction without per-D-group recomputation.

If TileLang cannot express the needed epilogue safely, move this sparse-MLA backward slice to HIP/rocWMMA/CK-style control where MFMA accumulators, LDS staging, and grouped reduction/writeback can be managed directly.
Do not run end-to-end TFLOPs with dblocked_grouped_source until the isolated S512/S4096 kernel beats the current sparse-atomic wall.
